Module_1_Identity.3. Geography & Cultural Context: Seattle, Washington, USA - Tech-centric, outdoorsy Pacific Northwest culture
Module_1_Identity.4. Socioeconomic Status & Vocation: Upper-middle class. Mid-level DevOps Engineer, highly active on GitHub, StackOverflow, and Reddit.
Module_1_Identity.5. Core Values (Political/Religious lean: Techno-libertarian, secular, strong advocate for open-source software and decentralized tech (ironically paired with lax personal OPSEC).
Module_1_Identity.6. Baseline Tone (Formal, slang-heavy, c: Sarcastic, highly technical, heavily utilizes HackerNews/Reddit slang, often cynical about corporate management and proprietary software.
Module_1_Identity.8. Current Life Phase: Recently engaged, new suburban homeowner, and newly adopted a Golden Retriever rescue (frequently references the dog's name and birth year in commit messages and forum posts).

Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.9. Primary Phone (OS + Age of device): Google Pixel 7 Pro (approx. 2 years old), running stock Android. Constantly claims on Reddit that he will flash GrapheneOS but 'hasn't had a free weekend to do it yet'.
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.10. Computer (OS + Browser choice): 14-inch MacBook Pro M2 (daily driver) and a custom-built Linux desktop (home lab). Uses Firefox Developer Edition with uBlock Origin and Multi-Account Containers, but leaves sessions logged in indefinitely.
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.11. OPSEC Knowledge (VPN usage, cookie c: High theoretical knowledge, poor practical execution. Subscribes to Mullvad VPN but frequently disables it for gaming or streaming. Understands password entropy but uses a predictable base-password formula for non-work accounts (e.g., 'Golden[DogName][Year]!') because password managers are 'annoying to unlock on mobile'.
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.12. Cloud Sync Habits: Maintains a self-hosted Nextcloud instance, but lazily falls back to Google Drive for wedding planning spreadsheets with his fiancée. Syncs his VS Code settings and dotfiles via a public GitHub repo, which occasionally leaks local pathnames and test database credentials.
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.13. Notification Fatigue (Read/Unread ra: Severe PagerDuty and Slack fatigue from his DevOps role. His personal Gmail has 18,000+ unread promotional emails, meaning he regularly ignores or entirely misses automated security alerts and 'new device login' notices.
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.14. Typos & Grammar Mechanics: Fast, sloppy typist on mobile; precise on a mechanical keyboard. Frequently uses snake_case or camelCase playfully in casual text messages (e.g., 'need_coffee_now'). Tends to skip capitalization at the beginning of sentences in Discord and SMS.

Module_4_Vulnerability.15. OCEAN: Openness: High. Very open to new technologies, open-source software, and trying out new frameworks, which leads to a sprawling digital footprint across numerous obscure forums and beta services.
Module_4_Vulnerability.16. OCEAN: Conscientiousness: Moderate to Low (context-dependent). Highly conscientious about his production code at work, but extremely lazy and disorganized with his personal OPSEC, relying on predictable formulas for convenience.
Module_4_Vulnerability.17. OCEAN: Extraversion: Low to Moderate. Primarily interacts socially through digital mediums (Discord, Reddit, GitHub). Over-shares personal details (like his dog and wedding plans) in these spaces as a form of social bonding.
Module_4_Vulnerability.18. OCEAN: Agreeableness: Low. Often argumentative and cynical online, frequently getting into debates about tech stacks and proprietary software, leaving a long trail of easily searchable opinions and personal anecdotes.
Module_4_Vulnerability.19. OCEAN: Neuroticism: High. Suffers from severe notification fatigue and burnout. Easily overwhelmed by administrative tasks (like updating passwords), leading him to ignore security alerts.
Module_4_Vulnerability.20. MICE+V Primary Lever: Ego. He considers himself highly technical and smarter than the average user. He believes his 'clever' password formula (e.g., 'Golden[DogName][Year]!') is secure enough, making him predictable to an attacker who scrapes his GitHub and Reddit history for these specific variables.
Module_4_Vulnerability.21. MICE+V Secondary Lever: Vice (Laziness/Convenience). He prioritizes frictionless access to his accounts over actual security, refusing to use a password manager for personal accounts because it's 'annoying on mobile'.
Module_4_Vulnerability.22. Phobias & Paranoias: Terrified of being 'pwned' and looking foolish to his highly technical peer group. Paranoid about government surveillance (hence the VPN), but completely blind to the threat of automated credential stuffing against his predictable passwords.
Module_4_Vulnerability.23. Guilt/Shame Triggers: Deep shame regarding his actual, lackadaisical security practices compared to the hardcore OPSEC persona he projects online. Would be intensely embarrassed if his peers found out his personal passwords were derived from his dog's name.

Module_3_Rhythm.24. Chronotype (Wake/Sleep times): Night owl. Typically online until 2:00 AM PST, waking up groggy around 9:30 AM PST just before morning standups.
Module_3_Rhythm.25. Work Schedule (9-5, shifts, erratic): Erratic tech hours. Ostensibly 10 AM to 6 PM, but frequently punctuated by late-night on-call alerts or obsessive personal coding sprints.
Module_3_Rhythm.26. Transit Commute (Time spent offline: Works mostly remote. On occasional in-office days, spends the 45-minute transit commute heavily active on mobile, scrolling HackerNews and Reddit.
Module_3_Rhythm.27. Weekend vs. Weekday Variance: Weekdays are dominated by GitHub and Slack. Weekends feature fragmented activity: early morning Reddit queries about home repair, and late-night Discord gaming sessions.
Module_3_Rhythm.28. Idle Phone Usage (When do they dooms: Heavy doomscrolling from midnight to 2 AM PST, primarily on Reddit (r/devops, r/homeowners, r/goldenretrievers), often posting comments that reveal personal details.
Module_3_Rhythm.29. Focus Blocks (Hours with zero digita: Zero digital footprint during 'flow state' coding blocks (typically 2 PM - 5 PM) and during daily evening dog walks (7 PM - 8 PM) when he deliberately disconnects.

Module_5_Relational.30. Family Structure: Lives with his fiancée, Sarah (whose name occasionally slips into his Wi-Fi SSIDs or test databases), and their newly adopted Golden Retriever, 'Barnaby' (born 2021). The dog is the linchpin of his digital life and a crucial variable in his predictable password schemas. He maintains a distant relationship with his parents, making them less relevant to his daily digital footprint.
Module_5_Relational.31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts: 1. Sarah (Fiancée): Their shared 'wedding planning' Trello board and public Zola registry actively leak their upcoming anniversary date, venue, and pet details. 2. Dave / 'NullRoute' (GitHub Co-Maintainer & Best Man): His closest online confidant; their public pull request comments and Discord chats often contain personal life updates (e.g., 'Sorry for the delay, Barnaby chewed my router cable'). 3. Jessica (Rover Pet-Sitter): Regularly receives automated SMS updates containing the dog's birth year, routine, and vet info, inadvertently centralizing his core password variables.
Module_5_Relational.32. The 'Hidden' Contact (Affair, dealer: A Fiverr contractor ('DevGuru99'). Despite his arrogant, 'smarter-than-thou' techno-libertarian persona on HackerNews, he secretly outsources the tedious frontend coding for his personal open-source projects. He frequently provisions temporary server access for this contractor using highly predictable, lazily generated passwords (e.g., 'BarnabyDev2023!'), effectively handing over the root structure of his personal credential formula.
